name: Nile Basin
depletionRate: High — Egypt uses 97% of its Nile allocation
description: The Nile sustains 250 million people across 11 countries in the world's longest river basin. Egypt depends on the Nile for 97% of its water supply. Ethiopia's Grand Ethiopian Renaissance Dam (GERD) — Africa's largest hydropower project — has reshaped basin geopolitics. With Egypt's population projected to reach 160 million by 2050, per-capita water availability is dropping below the UN scarcity threshold of 1,000 m³/year.
